summary.en: Both Chinese and English drop repeated or obvious elements to stay concise. Ellipsis splits into lexical (articles, prepositions, infinitives, clause markers) and syntactic (simple, compound, and complex sentences).
summary.zh: 中英文说话写作时都会省略重复或显而易见的成分，让表达更简洁。省略分为词法上的省略（冠词、介词、不定式、引导词）和句法上的省略（简单句、并列句、复杂句）。

heading.en: Dropping the article before a unique title
heading.zh: 独一无二称号前的冠词省略

body.en: Before a one-of-a-kind title or office — Chairman, President, Head — that is unique within its scope, the definite article 'the' can be dropped, because there is usually only one such position.
body.zh: 在某个范围内独一无二的称号或身份前，定冠词可以省略。比如 Chairman、President、Head 这样的职位往往就那么一个，所以可以省略前面的 The。

concepts.sourceRef: Chairman, President, Head这样的职位往往就那么一个

heading.en: The second article in a coordinated pair
heading.zh: 并列名词中的第二个冠词

body.en: When two coordinated nouns share the same article, the second article can be dropped as long as it causes no ambiguity. Beware: 'a teacher and video creator' is one person; 'a teacher and a video creator' is two — keeping or dropping the article changes the meaning.
body.zh: 两个并列的名词前有相同的冠词时，在不引发歧义的情况下，第二个冠词可省略。但要注意：a teacher and video creator 指同一个人，a teacher and a video creator 则指两个人——有无冠词意思不同，不能随意省略。

body.en: The most common preposition omission is the time preposition. 'I ate ten carrots that day' drops 'on' before 'that day'; 'I worked sixty hours last week' drops 'for' before the hours and 'in' before 'last week'.
body.zh: 最常见的省略介词情况就是时间介词。I ate ten carrots that day 省略了 that day 前的 on；I worked sixty hours last week 省略了 sixty hours 前的 for 和 last week 前的 in。

concepts.sourceRef: 最常见的省略介词的情况就是时间介词

body.en: In an adjective (relative) clause, a relative pronoun acting as object can be dropped: 'This is the rabbit we met yesterday.' In an object clause, the 'that' can be dropped: 'The rabbit said carrots were tasty.'
body.zh: 定语从句里，关系代词在从句中作宾语时可省略：This is the rabbit we met yesterday。宾语从句中的 that 也可以省略：The rabbit said carrots were tasty。

concepts.sourceRef: 关系代词在从句中做宾语可以省略

body.en: Imperatives drop the subject 'you' — that is their hallmark: 'Open the door, please.' Exclamations also drop heavily: 'What a smart rabbit!' omits 'He is'; 'How time flies!' omits the adverb 'fast'.
body.zh: 起始句（祈使句）省略主语 you，这是它的典型特征：Open the door, please。感叹句中的省略也很常见：What a smart rabbit! 省略了 He is；How time flies! 省略了副词 fast。

concepts.sourceRef: 省略主语 you 是起始句的典型特征

body.en: Coordination implies similar meaning, so repeated parts in the second clause drop out. The repeated subject goes ('The rabbit went home and ate a carrot'), and so can a repeated verb+object ('The rabbit has eaten the carrot, but the wolf hasn't').
body.zh: 并列暗示相似的意思，所以第二个分句中重复的部分可省略。重复的主语省略：The rabbit went home after work and ate a carrot；重复的谓语动词加宾语也可省略：The rabbit has eaten the carrot, but the wolf hasn't。

concepts.sourceRef: 并列句中的第二个或者后几个成分中，重复的部分就可以省略

heading.en: Ellipsis in complex sentences — the subordinate clause

body.en: In a complex sentence, ellipsis can hit the subordinate clause. After 'When young', the subject and 'be' are both dropped, and 'You are smarter than I' is short for 'than I am smart'. The missing info is recoverable from the main clause.
body.zh: 复杂句中，省略可发生在从句里。When young 省略了主语和 be 动词；You are smarter than I 其实是 than I am smart 的省略。省略的信息能从主句还原出来。

concepts.sourceRef: 其他类型的状语从句也可以省略

examples.text: Wolf is Head of our group.

note.en: 'Head' is a unique office, so 'the' before it is dropped — 'Wolf is the Head of our group.'
note.zh: Head 是独一无二的职位，所以前面的 the 省略了——完整为 Wolf is the Head of our group。

examples.text: Both the rabbit and wolf will be invited to the party.

note.en: The two coordinated nouns share 'the', so the second article is dropped — full form 'Both the rabbit and the wolf'.
note.zh: 两个并列名词共用 the，第二个冠词省略——完整为 Both the rabbit and the wolf。

examples.text: I ate ten carrots that day.

note.en: The time preposition 'on' before 'that day' is dropped — full form 'I ate ten carrots on that day.'
note.zh: that day 前的时间介词 on 省略了——完整为 I ate ten carrots on that day。

examples.text: This is the rabbit we met yesterday.

note.en: The relative pronoun acting as the object of 'met' is dropped — full form 'the rabbit that/whom we met yesterday'.
note.zh: 作 met 宾语的关系代词省略了——完整为 the rabbit that/whom we met yesterday。

note.en: An imperative: the subject 'you' is dropped — that is the hallmark of imperatives.
note.zh: 祈使句：省略了主语 you，这是祈使句的典型特征。

examples.text: The rabbit went home after work and ate a carrot.

note.en: The repeated subject of the second clause ('The rabbit') is dropped before 'ate'.
note.zh: 第二个分句重复的主语 The rabbit 在 ate 前被省略了。

examples.text: The rabbit has eaten the carrot, but the wolf hasn't.

note.en: The repeated verb+object 'eaten the carrot' is dropped after 'hasn't'.
note.zh: 重复的谓语动词加宾语 eaten the carrot 在 hasn't 后被省略了。

examples.text: When young, the rabbit went to school every day.

note.en: The time clause drops both subject and 'be' — full form 'When the rabbit was young, he went to school every day.'
note.zh: 时间状语从句省略了主语和 be 动词——完整为 When the rabbit was young, he went to school every day。

prompt.en: Which phrase refers to ONE person (a single individual who is both)?
prompt.zh: 哪个短语指的是同一个人（既是老师又是制作者）？

text.en: a teacher and a video creator
text.zh: a teacher and a video creator

text.en: the teacher and the video creator
text.zh: the teacher and the video creator

explain.en: Dropping the second article ('a teacher and video creator') marks one person with both identities; repeating it marks two people.
explain.zh: 省略第二个冠词（a teacher and video creator）表示一个人兼具两种身份；重复冠词则表示两个人。

prompt.en: Restore the dropped time preposition: 'I worked sixty hours last week' → 'I worked ____ sixty hours in last week.'
prompt.zh: 补回省略的时间介词：'I worked sixty hours last week' → 'I worked ____ sixty hours in last week.'

explain.en: 'For' before the duration 'sixty hours' is dropped in the concise version; 'in' before 'last week' is also dropped.
explain.zh: 简洁说法省略了时长 sixty hours 前的 for；last week 前的 in 也被省略。

prompt.en: In 'The rabbit said carrots were tasty', what has been dropped?
prompt.zh: 在 'The rabbit said carrots were tasty' 中，省略了什么？

explain.en: The 'that' introducing the object clause is dropped — full form 'The rabbit said that carrots were tasty.'
explain.zh: 引导宾语从句的 that 被省略了——完整为 The rabbit said that carrots were tasty。

quizzes.sentence: When was young, the rabbit went to school every day.
quizzes.fix: When young, the rabbit went to school every day.

explain.en: In this clause both the subject and 'be' are dropped together; you can't leave 'was' stranded. Use 'When young' (or write it out fully as 'When the rabbit was young').
explain.zh: 这种从句要把主语和 be 动词一起省略，不能只剩 was。应说 When young（或写全为 When the rabbit was young）。

prompt.en: Why can the subject 'you' be dropped in 'Open the door, please'?
prompt.zh: 为什么 'Open the door, please' 可以省略主语 you？

text.en: Because it is an imperative, where dropping 'you' is the hallmark
text.zh: 因为它是祈使句，省略 you 是其典型特征
因为 door 才是真正的主语
因为 please 代替了主语

explain.en: Imperatives address the listener directly, so the subject 'you' is understood and dropped.
explain.zh: 祈使句直接对听者说话，主语 you 不言自明，因此省略。

prompt.en: 'The rabbit likes carrots, and the wolf ____ apples.' What word is dropped here in the concise version?
prompt.zh: 'The rabbit likes carrots, and the wolf ____ apples.' 简洁说法省略了哪个词？

explain.en: The repeated verb 'likes' is dropped in the second clause — 'The rabbit likes carrots, and the wolf apples.'
explain.zh: 第二个分句省略了重复的动词 likes——The rabbit likes carrots, and the wolf apples。

note.en: Spoken ellipsis: 'I ate' (subject + verb) is dropped, leaving only the object.
note.zh: 口语省略：省略了 I ate（主语加谓语），只留下宾语。

note.en: Only the subject remains; 'ate the carrot' (verb + object) is dropped.
note.zh: 只留下主语；ate the carrot（谓语加宾语）被省略。

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: Would you like to eat a carrot?

note.en: Part of the object infinitive is dropped but 'to' is kept — 'I'd like to (eat a carrot).'
note.zh: 省略了宾语不定式的一部分，但保留了 to——I'd like to (eat a carrot)。
